nuggets.headline: Pete Rango uses the EP format not just for releasing songs, but for creating self-contained collaborative worlds with a single artist.
nuggets.text: While many artists use features to add variety, Pete Rango's 2022 release "Savage Planet" is a different beast. Every single track is a collaboration with the artist Xmane, effectively making the entire record a dedicated duo project that provides a deep dive into one specific creative partnership.

source.title: Download Pete Rango album songs: Savage Planet | Boomplay Music

nuggets.headline: The opening track's length isn't for show; it serves a specific structural purpose as a mood-setting overture for the entire project.
nuggets.text: As the opening and longest song on the three-track EP, "Oms at Play" acts as a sonic mission statement. Its four-minute runtime is used to deliberately establish the hazy, atmospheric palette that defines the project, functioning more like an ambient preamble than a conventional pop song.

source.title: Download Pete Rango album songs: Savage Planet | Boomplay Music

nuggets.headline: If the textured, genre-blurring production of 'Oms at Play' is what drew you in, you need to hear the sonic architecture of Mk.gee.
nuggets.text: For a similar blend of intimate R&B vocals, hazy synth pads, and intricately layered guitar work, check out multi-instrumentalist Mk.gee. His track "Are You Looking Up" is a perfect entry point, capturing that same feeling of a song that's both meticulously produced and deeply personal, blurring the line between bedroom pop and sophisticated soul.

source.title: Download Pete Rango album songs: Savage Planet | Boomplay Music

artistSummary: Pete Rango is an independent artist crafting a sound at the intersection of lo-fi R&B, dream pop, and atmospheric electronica. His work, particularly on EPs like 'Savage Planet', often eschews solo songwriting in favor of deep, project-spanning collaborations that build cohesive sonic worlds.
